prompt:
**Prototype redesign**: re-skin all of flux's UI to match the visual language of the `balance` app (`gunk-dev/balance`, also at `~/hack/measure`). This is a **design exploration PR** — the user wants to preview it and react. No functional changes; tests must still pass.

## The balance design language (extracted from `~/hack/measure/client/src/index.css` and `App.tsx`)

**Palette** (these are the source of truth — port them exactly):
- `sage` `#dce3d0` — app background (light, muted green)
- `cream` `#f4f3ea` — primary card surface (warm off-white)
- `sand` `#e8e6dd` — secondary surface (warm beige)
- `forest` `#4a534b` — primary text + accent (deep muted green-grey)
- `mint` `#a2b8ad` — accent card / call-to-action (soft sage-green)

**Typography:** Inter only, weights 400/500/600. Drop JetBrains Mono entirely. Body has no monospace anywhere in balance.

**Style signatures** (apply consistently across all components):
- **Big border-radius**: cards use `rounded-[2rem]`; full-screen frames use `rounded-[3rem]`. Buttons + inputs use `rounded-2xl` or `rounded-full`. Avoid the small `rounded`/`rounded-md` look.
- **Very soft shadows**: `shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgba(0,0,0,0.04)]` on cards. Avoid hard borders — let surfaces float on shadow.
- **Uppercase + heavy letter-spacing on labels/headings**: `text-xs tracking-[0.2em] uppercase font-medium` for app/section labels; `text-sm tracking-[0.1em] uppercase font-medium` for inline labels. Body copy is sentence-case, no tracking.
- **Restrained color use**: 90%+ of the UI is sage/cream/forest. Mint is reserved for call-to-action surfaces. No bright accents elsewhere.
- **No icons from external libs**: balance uses `lucide-react` but flux is **Preact**. Do NOT add `lucide-react` (React-only). Use inline SVG paths copied from lucide.dev (single-stroke, `strokeWidth={1.5}`) for icons — or unicode glyphs (✓ × ←) where adequate. Keep icon count low.

**Layout note — desktop framing:** balance wraps the whole UI in a 390×844 mobile-phone-shaped frame centered on desktop with a thick `rounded-[3rem]` border. Flux runs on Android via Capacitor (full viewport) and as a PWA. **Skip the phone-frame** — keep flux's existing full-viewport responsive layout. The redesign is about *materials* (color/type/shadow/radius), not the desktop framing trick.

## Both color modes required

The user chose to keep flux's existing dark/light mode toggle.

- **Light mode**: balance's palette verbatim. `body` background = `sage`. Primary cards = `cream`. Text = `forest`. Accent cards = `mint`.
- **Dark mode**: derive a dark variant that **preserves balance's warm/earthy spirit** — do NOT revert to cold grey. Suggested values (refine as needed):
  - bg: deep forest, e.g. `#2a302b` (very dark muted green)
  - card: slightly lighter forest, e.g. `#363d37`
  - soft surface: `#3f4740`
  - primary text: `cream` (~`#f4f3ea` slightly dimmed for AA contrast)
  - secondary text: muted mint (`#a2b8ad` at lower alpha)
  - accent: mint stays the same (or shift slightly cooler to pop on dark)
  - The vibe should feel like a balance-skinned dark mode, not flux's current Pareto-grey aesthetic.

Use CSS variables on `:root` and `[data-mode='light']`/`[data-mode='dark']` so the toggle in Settings continues to work. Keep the existing toggle UI (just retheme it).

## Files in scope

Restyle **every visible surface**:

- `tailwind.config.js` — replace the `flux:` color tokens with balance-derived tokens. You can either (a) repoint the existing `flux-bg`/`flux-card`/etc names to new CSS variable values, or (b) introduce new token names (`sage`/`cream`/`forest`/`mint`) alongside or replacing the `flux-*` ones. **Recommend (a)** for minimal churn — keep the `flux-*` Tailwind classes throughout the codebase, just change what they resolve to. This makes the diff much smaller.
- `src/index.css` — replace all theme tokens. Drop the existing OKLCH grey scale. Drop the `font-mono` registration if any. Body font-family stays Inter (no font swap needed). `-webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent` stays.
- `src/App.tsx` — restyle the tab nav at the bottom. Currently uses a flat `border-t` divider; consider a softer floating-pill nav that feels more like balance (use your judgment; the goal is the *feel*, not a direct copy).
- `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx` — restyle the header, progress bar, day navigation, and bottom toolbar. The data structure is unchanged; just visuals.
- `src/tabs/Workouts/components/ExerciseCard.tsx` — the biggest restyle target. Each exercise card should feel like a balance card: cream background, rounded-[2rem], soft shadow, uppercase tracking-wide section labels, mint accent for the Done button when active.
- `src/tabs/Workouts/components/Timer.tsx` — restyle the timer widget. Balance uses soft mint accents for active states; consider similar.
- `src/tabs/Workouts/components/VideoModal.tsx` — restyle modal chrome.
- `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` — restyle the three Data/Appearance/LLM cards.
- `src/tabs/Meditate.tsx` and `src/tabs/Checkin.tsx` — these are stubs; restyle the empty-state to feel intentional rather than placeholder. A centered uppercase label + a balance-style decorative element.

## Constraints

- **Functional behavior unchanged.** No state changes, no logic changes, no removed buttons. Just visuals.
- **No new runtime dependencies.** No lucide-react, no icon libs. Inline SVG only.
- **Tailwind 3 stays.** Don't try to upgrade to Tailwind 4 (balance is v4; flux is v3 — porting v3 is sufficient).
- **All existing `data-testid` attributes preserved.** Don't break tests.
- **Preserve accessibility**: maintain text contrast (AA), keep `aria-selected`/`role` attributes on tab nav, keep focus outlines visible (even if you restyle them).

## Validation

- `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test --project=chromium` — full e2e suite passes
- `nix develop --command npm run typecheck` — no TS errors
- `nix develop --command bash -c \"npm ci && npm run build\"` — production build succeeds
- Spot-check both modes manually if possible (open Settings, toggle dark/light) and confirm both feel balanced.

## PR meta

**Title:** "prototype: re-skin UI to match balance design language (preview)"

**Body:** make it explicit this is a **design prototype for preview** — not final. Include:
- The five color tokens (sage/cream/sand/forest/mint) with hex values
- A note that dark mode was derived (since balance is light-only) — call out the specific choices so the user can react
- A note that no functional changes were made; all logic, state, and tests untouched
- An invitation for the user to tweak specific surfaces ("if X feels too soft / too rounded, tell me which token to dial")
- A reminder that the existing dark/light toggle in Settings still works
